channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Cesar Chavez abused and raped women and girls, NYT investigation says
item.description: A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.
item.content:encoded:
A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.

(Image credit: George Brich)
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Six seasons and a sequel: 'Peaky Blinders' is easy to consume and impossible to forget
item.description: Tommy Shelby's estranged son has taken control of the old gang and is making new and dangerous moves and alliances. Cillian Murphy stars in this gripping movie-length Peaky Blinders sequel.
item.content:encoded:
Tommy Shelby's estranged son has taken control of the old gang and is making new and dangerous moves and alliances. Cillian Murphy stars in this gripping movie-length Peaky Blinders sequel.

(Image credit: Robert Viglasky Photography)
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: 'Nonesuch' author Francis Spufford explains the 'Blitz spirit' of 1940s London
item.description: Spufford's new novel centers a young woman in sexist World War II England as she navigates romance, tries to survive the nightly bombings of the Blitz and fights time-traveling fascists.
item.content:encoded: Spufford's new novel centers a young woman in sexist World War II England as she navigates romance, tries to survive the nightly bombings of the Blitz and fights time-traveling fascists.
item.dc:creator: Sam Briger

item.title: A mysterious floral artist has taken over the New York Botanical Garden
item.description: Mr. Flower Fantastic is a graffiti artist turned floral designer who keeps his identity a secret. His new show is an ode to NYC in orchids. Oh, and did we mention he's allergic to flowers?
item.content:encoded:
Mr. Flower Fantastic is a graffiti artist turned floral designer who keeps his identity a secret. His new show is an ode to NYC in orchids. Oh, and did we mention he's allergic to flowers?

(Image credit: New York Botanical Garden)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: Do you understand this billboard? If not, that's the whole point
item.description: San Francisco's streets are plastered with cryptic ads from AI startups. The strategy is intentional — but it's not without cost.
item.content:encoded:
San Francisco's streets are plastered with cryptic ads from AI startups. The strategy is intentional — but it's not without cost.

(Image credit: Chloe Veltman)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: She's won 24 Paralympic medals. But Oksana Masters wants to talk about times she lost
item.description: Oksana Masters leaves Italy with five new para Nordic skiing medals, extending her reign as the most decorated U.S. Winter Paralympian. She competes in summer sports too and is already eyeing LA 2028.
item.content:encoded:
Oksana Masters leaves Italy with five new para Nordic skiing medals, extending her reign as the most decorated U.S. Winter Paralympian. She competes in summer sports too and is already eyeing LA 2028.

(Image credit: Buda Mendes)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Geopolitics may test the World Cup — a new book draws lessons from the past
item.description: Countries all around the world will soon send players to the U.S. to compete in one of soccer's biggest events. Roger Bennett explores how past competitions met cultural and geopolitical moments.
item.content:encoded: Countries all around the world will soon send players to the U.S. to compete in one of soccer's biggest events. Roger Bennett explores how past competitions met cultural and geopolitical moments.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Pierre

item.title: From Descartes to punk rock, the letter X has an extraordinary history
item.description: The letter X can be a lot of things: rebellious, mysterious, religious. For this Word of the Week, we examine its origins and many uses.
item.content:encoded:
The letter X can be a lot of things: rebellious, mysterious, religious. For this Word of the Week, we examine its origins and many uses.

(Image credit: jclegg)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: 'Art Isn't Easy' author offers new insights into Stephen Sondheim's life and music
item.description: Biographer Daniel Okrent discusses Sondheim's approach to writing music and lyrics, his often toxic relationship with his mother and his work with mentors and collaborators.
item.content:encoded: Biographer Daniel Okrent discusses Sondheim's approach to writing music and lyrics, his often toxic relationship with his mother and his work with mentors and collaborators.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Team USA won the second-most medals at these Paralympics. See the standout moments
item.description: A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.
item.content:encoded:
A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.

(Image credit: Stefano Rellandini)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
